chapter_context.summary: Contrasts formal, hypocritical fasting with the true fast that pleases God: loosing bands of wickedness, feeding the hungry, and honoring the holy Sabbath.

outline.text: Rebuking Hypocritical Fasting and Exploitation of Labor
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Rebuking Hypocritical Fasting and Exploitation of Labor (58:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: True Fast: Loosing Bands, Feeding Hungry, Restoring Wastes
outline.explanation: This section focuses on True Fast: Loosing Bands, Feeding Hungry, Restoring Wastes (58:6-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Honoring and Finding Delight in the Holy Sabbath Day
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Honoring and Finding Delight in the Holy Sabbath Day (58:13-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Glory and Future Hope
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of glory and future hope, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 58, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
